mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips persistently for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger instances, forward 20d SPX returns are positive, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed the unconditional base rate by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) growth jointly signals broad-based consumer demand weakness that compounds beyond either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations for consumer-cyclical sectors are revised down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST YoY growth < 0 AND RSAFS YoY growth < 2%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 60d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from (or greater than) the unconditional mean, or the interaction term fails to add predictive power over either single-series model (incremental Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects early supply-side slack that markets price quickly, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-horizon momentum traders react to weakening manufacturing data.
failure_condition: The short-horizon correlation between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d SPX return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) across late-cycle sub-samples, or the effect reverses sign outside late-cycle windows without being flagged as regime-dependent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding a meaningful threshold signals an abrupt labor-market shock that raises recession probability assessments, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days to turn negative as risk premia widen.
failure_condition: Triggered payroll-decline episodes show forward 20d SPX returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or effect size is not distinguishable from noise (Rank IC < 0.03) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains flat or declining, the resulting negative real interest rate regime signals accommodative monetary conditions relative to inflation, supporting risk-asset valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; the effect requires both series moving jointly since either alone is ambiguous.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% AND FEDFUNDS flat or falling over 3 months
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns during joint-trigger episodes are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction model underperforms the single-series FEDFUNDS-only model in walk-forward Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on demand-momentum signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and forward 5d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following positive RSAFS prints are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms sustained economic expansion, reducing tail-risk premia and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but this relationship is conditional on the recovery regime and does not hold in stable mid-cycle periods.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% following a prior contraction quarter
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following qualifying GDPC1 prints in recovery regimes are not significantly higher than mid-cycle baseline returns (p > 0.05), or the relationship is equally strong outside recovery regimes, invalidating the regime-conditional claim.
